name: Valloré Viewpoint Trail
name_original: Mirador de Valloré – circular por las pasarelas del Río Guadalope (PR-TE 81)
description: Hike the Mirador de Valloré circular route, featuring impressive wooden walkways over the Río Guadalope and a viewpoint with panoramic views
region_intro: Hiking around Villarluengo offers access to a rugged landscape characterized by limestone spurs, deep gorges, and unique geological formations within Spain's Maestrazgo region. The area features dramatic ravines, towering limestone spires like the Órganos de Montoro, and significant natural monuments such as the Nacimiento del Río Pitarque. This mountainous terrain provides a diverse network of trails for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails in Villarluengo The most popular hiking route is Nacimiento del Río Pitarque Trail, a 5.6 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route leads to the spectacular emergence of the Pitarque River from a rock face. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valloré Viewpoint Trail, an easy 2.4 miles (3.9 km) path. This trail offers impressive vistas over the surrounding valleys and features the Mirador de Valloré. Local hikers also love the Maestrazgo Trail (GR 8): Tronchón to Mirambel, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Villarluengo is defined by dramatic limestone spires, deep river gorges, and the unique emergence of river sources. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding routes. The routes in Villarluengo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Villarluengo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villarluengo, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Villarluengo offers a diverse network of over 25 hiking trails. These include 12 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll, 12 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 4 difficult routes for experienced hikers looking for demanding terrain.

Q: What are some of the most notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Villarluengo?
A: The region is rich in dramatic natural beauty. You can witness the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Pitarque River&lt;/a&gt;, where the river emerges from a rock face. Don't miss the towering limestone spires of the Órganos de Montoro, or the impressive vistas from the Mirador de Valloré. The area also features unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Footbridges&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de Crespol Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in the Villarluengo area?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful water features. You can find a captivating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the route to Pozo de Invierno from Villarluengo is known for its beautiful waterfall carved into the rock, making water a central element of the hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Villarluengo?
A: The trails around Villarluengo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic limestone landscapes, the unique geological formations like the Órganos de Montoro, and the well-maintained paths that lead to natural wonders such as the Nacimiento del Río Pitarque.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Villarluengo?
A: Yes, Villarluengo offers several easy paths suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493780/vallore-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.9 km path that provides impressive vistas without being too strenuous, making it a great option for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Villarluengo?
A: Many trails in the Villarluengo region are suitable for dogs, allowing you to explore the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations if available.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Villarluengo?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes to enjoy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31453177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Footbridges – Valloré Footbridges loop from Montoro de Mezquita&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9 km circular hike. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Pitarque River – Source of the Pitarque River loop from Pitarque&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 16.3 km difficult route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villarluengo?
A: The Maestrazgo region, including Villarluengo, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially in the gorges, while winter can bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking trails that pass through Villarluengo?
A: Yes, the region is part of a larger network of trails. The GR 8 and its variants, as well as short-distance trails (PR), connect various municipalities and natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979655583/maestrazgo-trail-gr-8-tronchon-to-mirambel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maestrazgo Trail (GR 8): Tronchón to Mirambel&lt;/a&gt; is a 14.7 km section of this extensive network, offering a longer hiking experience through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Villarluengo?
A: The rugged terrain of Villarluengo is home to diverse wildlife. Hikers may have the chance to spot Spanish ibex (cabras hispánicas) on the rocky slopes and various raptors soaring overhead in the dramatic skies of the Maestrazgo region.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers near the trailheads in Villarluengo?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not always listed, Villarluengo and surrounding villages typically offer designated parking areas for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage or information boards upon arrival for the most convenient parking options for your chosen route.
tips: The best thing to do here is to catch your breath after the climb and rest with some wonderful views.

This interesting and picturesque route runs through the Guadalope River ravine and can be a real challenge for many. With sections of walkway hanging over the riverbed, some sections with ascent assisted by chains and sectors with steep walls, it is a path not suitable for those who suffer from vertigo, but for those who do not, it is a walk that you cannot miss!

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty level of the Mirador de Valloré – circular route?
faqs.answer: The difficulty of this route is mixed. While the sections along the Valloré Footbridges over the Río Guadalope are generally considered easy and accessible, the ascent to the Valloré Viewpoint is more demanding. This part involves steep sections, some equipped with cables and staples, requiring good physical condition.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The entire circular route is approximately 3.9 kilometers long and generally takes between 2 to 3 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable features. You'll experience the impressive wooden Valloré Footbridges that navigate the narrow sections of the Río Guadalope. The Valloré Viewpoint provides breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding canyons and the Río Guadalope straits. You'll also encounter dramatic geological formations like Boca Infierno and observe the Muela del Gabardal and Hoz Mala.

faqs.question: Is the Mirador de Valloré trail suitable for individuals with vertigo or a fear of heights?
faqs.answer: The ascent to the Valloré Viewpoint includes exposed sections along rock faces, equipped with cables and staples, and a vertical ascent aided by staples. These parts may not be suitable for individuals with vertigo or a significant fear of heights.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for potentially colder conditions and check for any ice on the equipped sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts in Montoro de Mezquita, Teruel. Specific parking information is usually available near the village center or designated trailhead areas. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most current parking options.

faqs.question: Is the PR-TE 81 trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog access is not explicitly stated in the provided trail details. Given the equipped sections and exposed areas, it's recommended to exercise caution and keep dogs on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail, especially since it's in Maestrazgo Geopark?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for hiking the PR-TE 81 trail itself. As it is located within the Maestrazgo Geopark, general park regulations apply, but typically, access to trails within geoparks is free. Always respect the natural environment and follow any posted rules.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll start on gentle paths, which gradually become steeper as you approach the rocky walls. Sections along the rock face, known as "fajas," can be exposed and are equipped with cables and staples. The route also includes wooden walkways over the Río Guadalope, and a vertical ascent aided by staples before reaching the viewpoint. The return journey involves similar equipped descents.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the Mirador de Valloré route, sections of this trail also intersect with the Camino de Montoro de Mezquita.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the Río Guadalope?
faqs.answer: The trail showcases rich riverside flora and fauna. Keep an eye out for griffon vultures soaring above the immense vertical walls and Hispanic goats on the rocky slopes. The river itself supports diverse aquatic life.
